meta_desc: Discover our complete range of inflatable cinema screens. Giant screens, airtight, economical - from 3m to 24m. Manufacturer since 1992.
photo_alt: A giant inflatable cinema screen by Hallucine set up in a park, with the audience taking their seats at dusk
hero_desc: From 3 meters to 24 meters wide. Our screens use technical fabrics from the kitesurfing and automotive industries to be up to 3 times lighter than the competition. 10-year warranty. Manufactured since 1992.
g1_desc: From 8m to 24m wide. Uses a permanent blower to maintain the structure. Automotive airbag polyamide fabric - up to 3× lighter than the competition. Ideal for large events, festivals, drive-ins.
g1_highlight: 8m to 24m - The world's largest inflatable screen
g2_desc: From 3m to 10m wide. Sealed-tube technology - just one inflation, no noise, no permanent electricity. Polyamide kitesurf fabric. Perfect for private parties, hotels, camping.
g3_desc: Affordable solution for tight budgets. Available with or without blower. Professional quality at an affordable price. Ideal for associations, communities and first equipment.
g4_desc: Detailed comparison chart: lightness, installation, warranty, carbon footprint. Find out why Hallucine outperforms the competition on every criterion.
g5_desc: Our new range of LED screens for projections visible even in broad daylight. High brightness, fast installation, outstanding image quality.
av1_desc: Technical fabrics for kitesurfing and car airbags instead of PVC tarpaulin.
av2_desc: Inflatable structure guaranteed for 10 years, projection canvas guaranteed for 5 years.
av3_desc: 5 to 45 minutes depending on size. No lifting equipment, even for 24m.
av4_desc: Over 60 countries delivered. Delivery time 2 to 4 weeks. Customs formalities handled.
faq_q1: What's the difference between a airtight screen and a wind screen?
faq_a1: The airtight screen uses a sealed air chamber: you inflate it once, and it stays in shape all evening without electricity or noise. The blower screen uses a permanent blower to keep the air flowing continuously - necessary for very large formats (over 8m) where air pressure must be constant.
faq_q2: Why are your screens 3× lighter than the competition?
faq_a2: Our competitors mainly use PVC tarpaulins. We use two technical fabrics: a airtight polyamide inspired by kitesurfing for the airtight range, and a high-tenacity polyamide from DuPont de Nemours (the fabric used in car airbags) for the blower range.
faq_q3: What screen size should I choose for my event?
faq_a3: The maximum comfortable viewing distance is about 6 to 8 times the screen width. For 100 people, a 3 to 5m screen is sufficient. For 500 people, aim for 8 to 12m. For 1000+ people, 15m or more.
faq_a4: Yes. Our inflatable screens are rated for winds of up to 38 km/h (6 Beaufort), in both the airtight and blower ranges. Beyond that, we recommend deflating the screen as a precaution — it only takes a few minutes.
faq_q5: Can we project in rear projection?
faq_a5: Yes, all our screens are designed for front and rear projection. The projection screen fabric is removable and reversible.
faq_a6: All our screens come with a 10-year warranty on the inflatable structure. The projection screen is guaranteed for 5 years.
cta_desc: Contact us for a personalized quote or consult our price list.
